Philosophy: A universal, non-restrictive crafting system governed by recipe discovery and material collection from combat-based activities rather than profession gating or stats RNG. Progress is replacement-based and fully deterministic.

CanonicalRules.Text: Crafting is a universal system available to all players without profession restrictions, governed by material acquisition and recipe knowledge.
Item tiers define acquisition method, not item quality or stat variance.
Items of Tier 4 and Tier 5 cannot be crafted under any circumstances.
Items are defined as abstract deterministic templates without narrative naming unless explicitly registered in canonical item database.
Recipes are unlocked exclusively through quests and structured narrative progression events.
Materials are exclusively obtained from combat-based World Activities (Hunts, Bosses, Elite encounters).
Crafting capability is universal; complexity is determined only by recipe requirements and material availability.
Crafting produces only base deterministic items with no enhancement or modification systems.

Tier_1.Name: Early game equipment
Tier_1.AcquisitionMethod: Primarily crafted, but may drop from basic monsters

Tier_2.Name: Low-mid game equipment
Tier_2.AcquisitionMethod: Primarily crafted, but may drop from basic monsters

Tier_4.Name: Late game equipment
Tier_4.AcquisitionMethod: Exclusive loot from high-tier content. Drop-only.
Tier_4.Source: Hunts, Bosses, Elite encounters, Demon / Dragon events

Tier_5.Name: Endgame apex equipment
Tier_5.AcquisitionMethod: Exclusive loot from apex-tier content. Extreme rarity drop-only.

UniversalCrafting.MaterialSources: Materials are exclusively obtained from combat-based World Activities (Hunts, Bosses, Elite encounters) under Loot System Bible rules. No gathering systems, environmental extraction, or passive world resource nodes exist.

EconomicAlignment.Tiers_1_2: Early game economy dominance via crafting
EconomicAlignment.Tiers_4_5: Endgame dependency shift toward loot systems, ensuring stable scarcity curve, controlled endgame inflation, and meaningful loot hierarchy
